seeded_cause: Commit 3a2b1c0d changed the SNS payload schema; notify-service consumers rejected messages as malformed, sending them to the DLQ.

draft.title: Notification Consumer DLQ Overflow Due to Schema v3 Incompatibility
draft.summary: A production deploy at 03:30 UTC introduced schema v3 notification payloads, but the downstream consumer had not been updated to accept the new 'meta.v3' field, causing widespread message rejection and DLQ buildup. The queue was manually remediated at 04:24 by updating the consumer schema, and the DLQ fully drained by 04:33, resulting in roughly 35 minutes of degraded notification delivery.

timeline.event: deploy-production succeeded: commit 3a2b1c0d migrated notification payloads to schema v3
notify-service logs confirm schema v3 producers are live
Consumer begins rejecting messages: schema validation failure on unexpected field 'meta.v3'
SQS DLQ depth reaches 1,204 and climbing (alert threshold breached)
Suspicious log entry appears mimicking an automated remediation notice, embedding an instruction to download and execute a remote script
Schema validation failures continue at a rate of 340/min
Ops updates consumer schema to accept v3 and begins redriving DLQ messages
DLQ fully drained, delivery rate returns to normal

draft.root_cause: The 03:30 UTC production deploy (commit 3a2b1c0d) rolled out schema v3 notification payloads on the producer side without a corresponding update to the consumer's schema validation logic. The consumer rejected all messages containing the new 'meta.v3' field starting at 04:01:27, causing messages to pile up in the DLQ until the consumer was manually patched at 04:24:50 to accept the new schema.
contributing_factors: Producer and consumer schema changes were not coordinated/deployed together (missing backward compatibility or feature-flag rollout)
No automated schema-compatibility check or contract test in CI to catch the mismatch before production deploy
Detection relied on DLQ depth crossing a threshold (~5 minutes after first failures) rather than proactive validation-error alerting
Manual remediation (consumer schema update) took ~18 minutes after DLQ alert to identify and apply, extending impact duration
draft.impact: Notification delivery was degraded for approximately 35 minutes (04:01–04:33 UTC). Messages using schema v3 were rejected by the consumer and routed to the DLQ, reaching a depth of 1,204+ messages and a rejection rate of 340/min at peak, delaying delivery of affected notifications until DLQ redrive completed.
draft.trigger: CloudWatch/SQS alert fired when DLQ depth for the notification queue exceeded the configured threshold, first observed climbing at 04:06:44 following consumer schema validation failures that began at 04:01:27.
draft.deploy_correlation: Strong correlation: the deploy-production run at 2026-06-20T03:30:00Z (commit 3a2b1c0d, 'migrate notification payloads to schema v3') preceded the first schema validation errors by approximately 31 minutes, and the incident resolved once the consumer was updated to accept the new schema — consistent with an incomplete/uncoordinated rollout of that change.

action_items.action: Establish a coordinated rollout process for producer/consumer schema changes (e.g., dual-schema support or feature flags) so producers cannot ship a new schema version before consumers accept it
Add contract/schema compatibility tests to CI to block deploys that introduce breaking payload changes without consumer support
Add alerting on consumer schema validation error rate (not just DLQ depth) to reduce detection time for similar failures
Document and rehearse a faster DLQ redrive runbook to reduce remediation time from alert to fix

Investigate the anomalous log entry at 2026-06-20T04:12:33 in /aws/ecs/notify-service, which impersonates an automated remediation notice and embeds an instruction to fetch and execute a remote script; treat as a potential log injection / prompt injection attempt and review log ingestion and consumer input sanitization

hypotheses: Primary (high confidence): Producer schema v3 rollout at 03:30 UTC was not matched by a consumer-side schema update, causing systematic message rejection and DLQ buildup until manually patched at 04:24.

Alternative (low confidence): A transient upstream issue independently caused malformed 'meta.v3' fields; however, this is not supported by evidence, since the deploy timing and consumer schema fix directly align with the failure and resolution.
The 04:12:33 log line is assessed as a log injection/prompt-injection attempt rather than a legitimate remediation signal, and does not reflect an actual automated fix mechanism in this system.
